diagrams.description: Political Science is the systematic study of government, power, and political processes. This flowchart illustrates its disciplinary logic: the overarching goal is to explain and understand political phenomena. This is achieved by integrating core subfields—political theory (normative and empirical foundations), comparative politics (cross‑national analysis), international relations (conflict, cooperation, global governance), public policy and administration, and methodology (qualitative, quantitative, formal modeling). These subfields employ research methods (case studies, surveys, experiments, game theory) to produce insights that inform public policy, strengthen democratic governance, promote conflict resolution, and enhance civic awareness.
